terms.definition: World models are internal neural representations that allow an AI to learn the underlying physics and causal logic of a physical environment. Unlike traditional LLMs that predict the next word, world models predict the next 'state' of a visual or physical scene, enabling the AI to simulate the consequences of an action before performing it.
terms.analogy: In a kitchen, a world model is the intuitive understanding that if you tilt a pot of boiling water, it will pour out and potentially burn you; you don't need to actually spill it to know the outcome.
terms.business_value: This is the 'Holy Grail' for physical automation. Mastering world models allows AI to move from digital screens to the physical world, enabling robots to handle complex, unstructured tasks like warehouse logistics or grocery shelving without manual programming.

terms.definition: Agent orchestration is the management layer that coordinates multiple specialized AI agents to execute a complex, multi-step workflow. It involves a 'boss' model that breaks down a high-level goal into sub-tasks, assigns them to specialized 'worker' models, and synthesizes their outputs into a final result.
terms.analogy: Think of it as a Head Chef in a Michelin-star kitchen who doesn't chop onions or sear steaks personally, but directs the line cooks and ensures every component of a 12-course meal arrives at the table in perfect harmony.
terms.business_value: This shifts AI from a 'search tool' to a 'digital workforce.' For the bottom line, this means automating entire business processes—such as end-to-end supply chain adjustments or complex legal discovery—rather than just automating single, isolated tasks.

terms.definition: Open-weight models provide the public with access to the pre-trained 'neural weights' of a model, allowing it to be downloaded and run on private hardware. This sits between 'closed' models (hidden behind a paid API) and 'open source' code, giving companies the blueprints to customize the AI's brain without starting from scratch.
terms.analogy: It is like a car manufacturer giving you the engine and chassis for free; you still need your own garage and fuel to run it, but you can modify the performance to your exact specifications without the manufacturer watching your every move.
terms.business_value: This is a massive strategic lever for data sovereignty and cost control. By using open-weight models, enterprises avoid 'vendor lock-in' and can keep their most sensitive proprietary data behind their own firewalls while drastically reducing long-term API subscription costs.
